series_metadata.series_description: records of advocates placed in facilities; includes reports and investigations regarding rights protection of individuals
facility policies and procedures to align with the requirements of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) SEE ALSO: Agency Policies (AGENCY MANAGEMENT), Contracts (LEGAL)

records created and/or received by a facility’s infection control program; includes investigative reports, surveillance records and logs, infection control reports NOTE: Records concerning employees who may have been or were exposed to a communicable disease should be transferred to the Personnel file

records created by Division of State Operated Healthcare Facilities to oversee data sharing and patient safety initiatives; includes event investigations, root cause analyses, and other related patient safety work product (PSWP)

series_metadata.series_title: Performance Improvement and Quality Assurance Records
series_metadata.series_description: incident analyses and reports; includes medication error reports, occurrence reports, and performance improvement plans

series_metadata.series_title: Performance Improvement and Quality Assurance Records
series_metadata.series_description: formal and informal data collection logs and records
includes master patient index (MPI) documenting patient registration, medical record number, and date(s) of admission; also includes admission and death registers
schedules for medical personnel including physicians, medical aides, nursing staff, and other support personnel who provide medical treatment SEE ALSO: , (below) Daily Staffing RecordsFloat Rosters
lists of all patients seen or scheduled to be seen by medical personnel and labs SEE ALSO: , (below) Daily Staffing RecordsFloat Rosters
records of adjustments to patients’ bills and insurance claims
report summarizing charges to daily in-patients
claim forms submitted to insurance companies; also includes reports summarizing unpaid insurance claims
logs listing payments made to healthcare providers and indicating the accounts to which they are posted; also includes disbursement reports and bad debt Medicare logs
records documenting paid balances and refunds for patients/residents receiving Medicaid/Medicare benefits
records documenting paid balances and refunds issued to patients/residents or insurance companies
vouchers for payments received from patients and insurance companies

records documenting the admission, discharge, and transfer of patients/residents; includes lists of patient’s name, age, sex, race, address, financial class, services received, admitting doctor, date admitted, date discharged, date transferred, floor, room, and other related information
resident clinical records for adults admitted to a nursing facility
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 10A NCAC 13D .2401 10A NCAC 28F .0605 Confidentiality G.S. § 122C-52 Retention 10A NCAC 13D .2402(a)

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the proper functioning of autoclave equipment; includes mechanical, chemical, and biological monitor notebooks and sterilizer logs and tests
logs and records detailing patient-related correspondence that is not part of the clinical record
records concerning dental treatments; includes tests and diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, treatment plans, and other related records

medical record index listing patients’ names, discharge dates, medical record numbers, dates of service, financial class, attending physicians’ names, procedures performed, diagnostic and procedural codes, and other related information
resident clinical records for those from birth to 18 years of age admitted to a nursing facility
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 10A NCAC 13D .2401 10A NCAC 28F .0605 Confidentiality G.S. § 122C-52 Retention 10A NCAC 13D .2402(b)

retention_rules.trigger_event: Last encounter : : * NOTE: Records must be retained patient reaches 19 years of age in addition this minimum time requirement

series_metadata.series_description: records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning NC residents diagnosed with tuberculosis or typhoid
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning diagnoses and case surveillance for other communicable diseases
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning the analysis of environmental samples for radioisotope presence
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning the bacteriologic, chemical, and fluoride analyses of water samples
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services about chronic disease and other health issues; includes incidence reporting and other surveillance methods
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services regarding immunizations administered in North Carolina
records concerning chain of custody of laboratory samples
records documenting all analytic systems activities; includes worksheets, specimen logs, and instrument printouts

records used to monitor the process by which blood products are made available and used; includes donor information and informed consent forms; records concerning the storage, distribution, and visual inspection of blood products; compatibility testing; component preparation; therapeutic bleedings; and immunizations; also includes blood collection and processing results, interpretations of tests, labeling, emergency releases of blood, and equipment calibration and performance checks; also includes transfusion reaction reports and complaints, investigations, errors and accident records, difficulties in blood typing reports, exposures to transmissible diseases, supplies and reagents, and final disposition reports of blood products
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 21 CFR 606.160 21 CFR 606.165 21 CFR 606.170 Confidentiality G.S. § 131E-97 Retention 21 CFR 606.160(d)

retention_rules.trigger_event: Complete : : NOTE: Records should be destroyed no less than records of processing have been completed or latest expiration date individual product, whichever occurs later

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning blood products with no expiration date
fine needle aspiration slides
stained slides; includes both negative/unsatisfactory and positive/suspicious samples
log of laboratory tests performed for clinical care
newborn screening collection forms
test requisitions and test authorizations

records used to attest the handling, preparation, processing, examination, and reporting of results of all proficiency testing; includes testing report forms, records documenting testing failures and corrective actions, and other related records

records documenting a laboratory’s quality control procedures; includes routine surveys and maintenance records, instrument calibrations, instrument/reagent correlations studies, quality control tests, silver recovery records, and other related records
standard operating procedures and policies related to lab testing
records produced by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner; includes request for analysis, chain of custody, data sheets, analyst’s worksheets, and other related records
tests on coronary arteries of adult patients; includes film and electronic images produced during procedure SEE ALSO: , (above) Adult Clinical RecordsPediatric Clinical Records

statistical sheets and logs documenting the number of patients given examinations during a given reporting period; includes patient names, names of attending physicians, type and number of examinations performed, and other related information
nuclear medicine exams and imaging diagnostic tests, including CAT scans, MRI scans, and x-rays; includes film and electronic images produced during tests
records concerning ambulance reports generated when the ambulance is used to transport a Division of State Operated Healthcare Facilities patient; includes ambulance run reports
assignments sheets specifying nurses’ floor assignments; includes nurses’ names and numbers of patients seen
records concerning individuals receiving services in the emergency department
checklists regarding equipment reliability on the code cart
lists of nurses sent to other units within the facility; includes nurse’s name, unit sent to, hours worked, and time spent
records used to track room usage; includes number of patients and number of rooms available
records concerning observation of patients in the facility less than 24 hours
lists of patient sitters
reports to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) describing adverse drug reactions
pharmaceutical waste manifests

records documenting the disposal or final disposition of all outdated, improperly labeled, adulterated, damaged, or unwanted controlled and non-controlled substances, or drug containers with worn, illegible, or missing labels
records listing who filled and/or checked a medication at time of issuing or dispensing and other related information

inventories of controlled and non-controlled substances; includes inventory reports, ancillary drug cabinet inventories, annual and biennial inventories, perpetual inventories, and other related records used to account for medication compounding and dispensing by pharmacies and locations outside the pharmacy; also includes inventories of drugs destroyed, their amounts, and when destroyed
Medicaid and Medicare Part D medication and billing records; includes electronic records used to maintain billing
insurance claim forms, confirmation or denial reports, remittance and status reports, and other related records submitted by pharmacies for reimbursement

series_metadata.series_title: Medication Storage and Environment Inspection Reports
series_metadata.series_description: results of medication storage areas inspected on a routine basis; also includes intravenous hood performance reports
prescription orders for controlled and non-controlled substances or other medication or devices that are not recorded in the patient’s medical record
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority G.S. § 90-85.30 G.S. § 90-85.35 Confidentiality G.S. § 90-85.36 Retention G.S. § 90-85.26 21 NCAC 46 .2302

series_metadata.series_title: Approval for Nursing Home Placement Records
series_metadata.series_description: completed forms approving patients’ placements in nursing homes

series_metadata.series_title: Children’s Developmental Service Agency (CDSA) Patient Clinical Records
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning admissions and services of CDSA clients of the Infant-Toddler Program; includes medical histories, results of eligibility determinations, evaluations and assessments, diagnostic goals, Individualized Family Service Plans, service notes, summaries of treatment received, responses to treatment, follow-up reports, and other related records

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center after 2 years. Records will be held for agency 10 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: records used to track patients receiving care through a facility’s social service program; includes patient’s name and address, room number, consultation date, placement information, and other related records

series_metadata.series_title: Referrals to County Department of Social Services
series_metadata.series_description: completed forms (PA-400) used as referrals to county department of social services for patients who may be eligible for Medicare
record of money paid to provider by Medicaid or Medicare
logs listing financial classifications applicable to patients
abstracts of codes used to track both patient billing and types of diseases diagnosed (used to report this information to insurance companies)
records used to track those patients who pay their bill without insurance or Medicare
lists summarizing transfers and discharges of patients
plans for utilization review
